title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Stop Overpaying for EBS I The Keys to AWS Optimization | S16 E3

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This livestream is a practical deep dive into Amazon EBS cost optimization, showing how to find overprovisioned volumes, right-size storage, and use the latest EBS volume types to align performance with cost. It also covers removing waste from unused snapshots and outdated volumes, demonstrates monitoring tools, and shares actionable strategies to lower EBS spending without sacrificing performance.
bullets: Identify and right-size overprovisioned EBS volumes to reduce cost.
Choose appropriate EBS volume types to match performance needs with cost efficiency.
Eliminate waste from unused snapshots, outdated volumes, and inefficient configs.
Demonstrations of monitoring tools for tracking storage utilization.
Real-world examples and actionable strategies to cut EBS spending without losing performance.
takeaways: Optimizing EBS sizing and volume types can materially lower AWS storage bills for data centers and cloud infrastructure.
Regularly cleaning unused snapshots and obsolete volumes prevents ongoing, avoidable costs.
Implementing monitoring and utilization tools enables continuous cost control and informed provisioning decisions.
Actionable, real-world techniques allow cost reduction while maintaining required performance levels.

items.title: Let's talk about valuations ft. Tim Deal

ai.summary: Based on the available description, valuation is getting a tech and AI glow-up and PwC demonstrates how it helps clients boost efficiency, quality, and consistency across the valuation process. The video emphasizes PwC's application of technology and AI to improve valuation workflows.
bullets: Valuation processes are being enhanced with technology and AI.
PwC positions itself as helping clients boost efficiency in valuations.
Emphasis on improving quality and consistency throughout the process.
Applies technology and AI across every step of valuation workflows.
Framed within business technology and innovation contexts.
takeaways: Faster, more efficient valuations can accelerate capital-allocation and project-approval timelines for grid and data center investments.
Improved quality and consistency in valuations increases confidence in investment and asset-management decisions.
Tech-enabled, standardized valuation outputs make comparisons across sites and scenarios easier and more reliable.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, in 2015 contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre parcel in upstate New York for $27,000 with the goal of building Airbnb properties. Over three years he commuted on weekends to build an A-frame cabin, added more cabins, and as of September 2025 the property has generated over $2.1 million in revenue and commands up to $700 per night.
bullets: 2015 purchase: five-acre property in upstate New York for $27,000.
Chris Broomfield, a full-time contractor, built an A-frame cabin by commuting on weekends over three years.
Additional cabins were added on the property and listed as short-term rentals.
As of September 2025 the property has brought in over $2.1 million and charges up to $700/night.
takeaways: A small rural land purchase can be converted into a high‑revenue short‑term rental business, highlighting potential concentrated economic activity in rural areas.
Growth of luxury short‑term rentals may increase demand for reliable local infrastructure and utility services in remote locations.
Individual developers working incrementally can scale a property over several years, suggesting gradual but sustained changes in local service needs.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the podcast discusses how operational changes—like moving production, distribution or teams—can constitute a restructuring from the perspective of transfer pricing and valuations. KPMG experts Monika Palmowska and Tomasz Regulski explain how to detect significant changes, associated reporting obligations (TPR, MDR), the role of timely documentation and common valuation pitfalls, and offer a practical checklist for firms.
bullets: Defines restructuring in transfer pricing context and how to spot material changes (including EBIT analysis).
Covers required documentation for restructurings and benefits of preparing it on time.
Outlines reporting duties such as Transfer Pricing Documentation (TPR) and Mandatory Disclosure Rules (MDR).
Discusses the role of valuations, frequent mistakes, and examples like moving production or distribution.
Addresses employee transfers, contract transfers, and provides a practical checklist of actions.
takeaways: Treat relocations of production/distribution or function shifts as potential transfer-pricing events and assess EBIT impact early.
Prepare transfer-pricing documentation ahead of time to reduce future compliance risk and administrative stress.
Ensure you understand and comply with TPR and MDR reporting obligations tied to restructurings.
Use robust valuations and watch for common valuation/contract-transfer pitfalls when changing operational footprints.
Include employee and contract transfer implications in restructuring plans and follow the podcast checklist for actions.
